title: Fast Entry Sheet — Currency Display and FX Transfer Dual Amount UI
description: Update FastEntrySheet and FastEntryViewModel to show the selected account's currency code next to the amount field. For Transfer transactions, detect when source and target accounts differ in currency and present a second amount field for the target amount.
execution_steps: Run 'loom start TASK-119' to lock active context
Run 'loom context TASK-119' to ingest requirements
Write failing tests in FastEntryViewModelTest (Red Phase):
- Selecting an account exposes its currencyCode in UI state
- Transfer with same-currency accounts: isFxTransfer = false, targetAmountCents = null
- Transfer with different-currency accounts: isFxTransfer = true, second amount field enabled
In FastEntryViewModel: add `val currencyCode` derived from selected account; add `val isFxTransfer` and `val targetAccountCurrency` for transfer mode; add `targetAmountCents` state
In FastEntrySheet: replace hardcoded '$' prefix on amount field with `currencyCode` from ViewModel state
In FastEntrySheet transfer section: when isFxTransfer == true, render a second OutlinedTextField labeled with targetAccountCurrency; otherwise hide it
Wire targetAmountCents into SaveTransferUseCase call (pass fromAccount + toAccount objects, not just IDs)
Add `@trace TASK-119` to FastEntrySheet.kt and FastEntryViewModel.kt
definition_of_done: Amount field prefix in FastEntrySheet shows the selected account's currencyCode, not '$'
For Transfer with same-currency accounts: only one amount field shown
For Transfer with different-currency accounts: two amount fields shown, each labeled with their account's currency
FX transfer cannot be saved with missing target amount (ViewModel validation)
FastEntryViewModelTest covers currency display and FX mode toggling and passes
